claimText: The Unified Lending Interface (ULI) is a Digital Public Infrastructure introduced by the RBI to enhance credit delivery.
evidenceExcerpt: Unified Lending Interface (ULI) is a Digital Public Infrastructure aiming to enhance credit delivery.
examUse: Statement-based question on RBI digital initiatives.
distractorHooks: Stating ULI is for deposit collection
Confusing ULI with UPI for retail payments

claimText: Certificate of Deposits (CDs) are negotiable money market instruments issued at a discount to their face value.
evidenceExcerpt: CDs are negotiable money market instruments issued at a discount to face value
examUse: Statement-based question on the features of money market instruments.
distractorHooks: Stating CDs are capital market instruments
Stating CDs are issued at a premium or face value with fixed interest

claimText: Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) and Local Area Banks (LABs) are excluded from issuing Certificate of Deposits (CDs).
evidenceExcerpt: Certificate of Deposits (CDs) are issued by Scheduled Commercial Banks, excluding Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks.
examUse: Statement-based question on eligible issuers of money market instruments.
distractorHooks: Stating RRBs and LABs are permitted to issue CDs
Stating only the RBI can issue CDs

claimText: The maturity period for Certificate of Deposits (CDs) ranges from 7 days to one year.
evidenceExcerpt: CDs are issued at a discount on face value with a maturity period ranging from 7 days to one year.
examUse: Statement-based question on the tenure of Certificate of Deposits.
distractorHooks: Stating the maturity period is 1 to 3 years
Stating the minimum maturity period is 14 days
